text:
### Expert help from one of our panel of solicitors

We know that many Advicenow users can’t afford to pay a solicitor but if you are trying to sort out finances on divorce, or are going to court about either the children or finances it will be really worthwhile to get a little if you possibly can. To help with this, we have teamed up with Resolution to provide a panel of family law solicitors that can advise you at the most important points of the process for a fixed fee. You can see up front exactly what areas of your case they can advise you on and how much their help will cost you - so that you can be certain you can afford it.

Even better, we have designed a process to make getting this help as cheap as possible for you, by making sure that you use the solicitor’s time as efficiently as possible. See Our affordable advice service for more help.

“

Great document, in plain language. Very useful & informative

“

Looking for help? then look no further, easy to understand and lots of information to guide through this difficult process. I am representing myself (litigant) in court and this is very helpful.

These other guides will be helpful too:

- How to agree where the children will live and when they will see their other parent
- Help on how to divide the home and money when you divorce
- Family mediation
- Going to court about where the children will live and when they will be with their other parent - if you really can't agree
- Help with understanding pensions on divorce
- Going to court for a fair financial settlement as part of your divorce - if you really can't agree
- Going to the family court

Please help us by telling us what you think about our content. You help us make it better, help other people know what was useful, and help us get funding to keep our website going.
